1: Why then should I think upon a maid?
2: And what what inheritance of the Almighty from on high?
3: And a strange punishment punishment to the workers of iniquity?
5: Or if my foot hath hasted to deceit;
6: That God may know mine integrity.
7: And if any blot hath cleaved to mine hands;
8: Yea, let my offspring be rooted out.
9: Or if if I have laid wait at my neighbour's door;
10: And let others bow down upon her.
11: Yea, it is is an iniquity to be punished by to be punished by the judges.
12: And would root out all mine increase.
14: And when he visiteth, what shall I answer him?
15: And did not one fashion us in the womb?
16: Or have caused the eyes of the widow to fail;
17: And the fatherless hath not eaten thereof;
18: And I have guided her from my mother's womb;)
20: And if if he were not not warmed with the fleece of my sheep;
21: When I saw my help in the gate:
22: And mine arm be broken from the bone.
23: And by reason of his highness I could not endure.
24: Or have said to the fine gold, Thou art Thou art my confidence;
25: And because mine hand had gotten much;
26: Or the moon walking in in brightness;
27: Or my mouth hath kissed my hand:
28: For I should have denied the God that is that is above.
29: Or lifted up myself when evil found him:
30: By wishing a curse to his soul.
31: Oh that we had of his flesh! we cannot be satisfied.
32: But But I opened my doors to the traveller.
33: By hiding mine iniquity in my bosom:
34: That I kept silence, and and went not out of the door?
35: And that that mine adversary had written a book.
36: And And bind it as as a crown to me.
37: As a prince would I go near unto him.
38: Or that the furrows likewise thereof complain;
39: Or have caused the owners thereof to lose their life:
40: The words of Job are ended.
